description: Parse URLs into components and reassemble them. Supports relative URL resolution and query string parsing.

functions.name: urlparse

params.name: scheme
params.description: Default scheme to use if the URL has none

params.name: allow_fragments
params.description: If False, fragment identifiers are not recognized and are parsed as part of the preceding component

returns.description: A 6-tuple named tuple: (scheme, netloc, path, params, query, fragment)

functions.description: Parse a URL into six components. The URL is split into scheme, network location, path, parameters, query, and fragment identifier.

functions.name: urlunparse

params.name: parts
params.description: A 6-tuple of (scheme, netloc, path, params, query, fragment) as returned by urlparse

returns.description: A URL string constructed from the components

functions.description: Construct a URL from a tuple of components as returned by urlparse(). This may result in a slightly different but equivalent URL if the original had unnecessary delimiters.

functions.name: urlsplit

params.name: scheme
params.description: Default scheme to use if the URL has none

params.name: allow_fragments
params.description: If False, fragment identifiers are not recognized

returns.description: A 5-tuple named tuple: (scheme, netloc, path, query, fragment)

functions.description: Parse a URL into five components. Similar to urlparse() but does not split the params from the path. Useful for URL schemes that do not use params.

functions.name: urlunsplit

params.name: parts
params.description: A 5-tuple of (scheme, netloc, path, query, fragment) as returned by urlsplit

returns.description: A URL string constructed from the components

functions.description: Construct a URL from a tuple of components as returned by urlsplit(). This may result in a slightly different but equivalent URL.

functions.name: urljoin

params.name: url
params.description: Relative or absolute URL to join with the base

params.name: allow_fragments
params.description: If False, fragment identifiers are not recognized

functions.description: Construct a full (absolute) URL by combining a base URL with a relative URL. Uses RFC 2396 semantics. If url is an absolute URL, the base is ignored.

functions.name: urldefrag

params.name: url
params.description: URL string that may contain a fragment identifier

returns.description: A tuple (defragmented_url, fragment). If the URL has no fragment, fragment is the empty string.

functions.description: Remove the fragment identifier from a URL. Returns a tuple of the URL without the fragment and the fragment itself.

functions.name: parse_qs

params.name: keep_blank_values
params.description: If True, include keys with blank values in the result

params.name: strict_parsing
params.description: If True, raise ValueError on parsing errors instead of silently ignoring them

returns.description: Dictionary mapping parameter names to lists of values

functions.description: Parse a query string and return a dictionary. Each key maps to a list of values. Data is returned as strings. Percent-encoded sequences are decoded.

functions.name: parse_qsl

params.name: keep_blank_values
params.description: If True, include keys with blank values in the result

params.name: strict_parsing
params.description: If True, raise ValueError on parsing errors instead of silently ignoring them

returns.description: List of (name, value) pairs as strings

functions.description: Parse a query string and return a list of (name, value) pairs. Useful when parameter order matters or when duplicate keys should be preserved in order.

classes.name: ParseResult
classes.description: Named tuple result of urlparse(). A 6-tuple with additional properties for accessing URL components. Supports indexing and attribute access.

methods.name: geturl
methods.description: Return the re-combined version of the original URL as a string.

fields.name: hostname
fields.description: Hostname from netloc, lowercased. None if not present.

fields.name: port
fields.description: Port number from netloc as integer. None if not present.

fields.name: username
fields.description: Username from netloc. None if not present.

fields.name: password
fields.description: Password from netloc. None if not present.

classes.name: SplitResult
classes.description: Named tuple result of urlsplit(). A 5-tuple similar to ParseResult but without the params component. Parameters are not split from the path.

methods.name: geturl
methods.description: Return the re-combined version of the original URL as a string.

fields.name: hostname
fields.description: Hostname from netloc, lowercased. None if not present.

fields.name: port
fields.description: Port number from netloc as integer. None if not present.

fields.name: username
fields.description: Username from netloc. None if not present.

fields.name: password
fields.description: Password from netloc. None if not present.
